name: Arena Validation & Difficulty Parameter Checks
theme: Boss arena enclosure validation, difficulty table compliance checks, complete validation report
features: F24: Playability Validator System (arena validation + difficulty parameters)

criteria.description: Validator checks that every boss arena has solid walls on all 4 sides (top, bottom, left, right) except the entry point
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect each stage result for an arenaResult section
Verify arenaResult includes an 'enclosure' check with PASS/FAIL
criteria.threshold: Each of the 13 stage results includes an arenaResult.enclosure field with PASS or FAIL status

criteria.description: Arena enclosure check verifies all 4 walls: top row is solid, bottom row is solid, left column is solid, right column is solid (entry point excluded)
testSteps: Review the arena enclosure validation function
Verify it checks top, bottom, left, and right boundaries of the boss arena for solid tiles
Verify the entry point (left side gap) is excluded from wall completeness checks
criteria.threshold: Code explicitly checks all 4 walls and excludes the entry point from the left wall check

criteria.description: Validator checks that every boss arena is at least 15 tiles wide
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect each stage's arenaResult for a 'minWidth' check
Verify arenaResult.minWidth includes the measured width and PASS/FAIL
criteria.threshold: Each stage result has arenaResult.minWidth with measured value and PASS/FAIL (PASS when >= 15 tiles)

criteria.description: Validator checks that every boss arena contains at least 2 platforms for player positioning
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect each stage's arenaResult for a 'platforms' check
Verify arenaResult.platforms includes the count and PASS/FAIL
criteria.threshold: Each stage result has arenaResult.platforms with count >= 2 for PASS

criteria.description: Validator checks that the arena entry point is accessible from the left side without requiring wall-jumps
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect each stage's arenaResult for an 'entry' check
Verify arenaResult.entry includes accessibility status and PASS/FAIL
criteria.threshold: Each stage result has arenaResult.entry with PASS/FAIL indicating left-side ground-level accessibility

criteria.description: Arena entry accessibility check verifies that the player can walk into the arena from the left at ground level (solid ground beneath entry gap, no wall-jump required)
testSteps: Review the entry accessibility validation function
Verify it checks for a gap in the left wall with solid floor beneath it
Verify it confirms walkable entry (not requiring wall-jump to enter)
criteria.threshold: Code checks for ground-level entry gap with solid floor support

criteria.description: Validator checks boss vulnerability windows against the difficulty table: World 1 = 3.0s, World 2 = 2.5s, World 3 = 2.0s, World 4 = 1.5s, Citadel = 1.5s
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect each stage's difficultyResult for 'vulnerabilityWindow' check
Verify the expected and actual values are reported per world
criteria.threshold: Each stage result has difficultyResult.vulnerabilityWindow with expected value matching the difficulty table for its world

criteria.description: The difficulty table for vulnerability windows is defined as a constant in the validator code with exact per-world values
testSteps: Review the validator code for a difficulty table constant
Verify World 1 = 3.0 (or 180 frames), World 2 = 2.5 (or 150 frames), World 3 = 2.0 (or 120 frames), World 4 = 1.5 (or 90 frames), Citadel = 1.5 (or 90 frames)
criteria.threshold: Difficulty table constant exists with exact per-world vulnerability window values

criteria.description: Validator checks boss HP against the difficulty table ranges: World 1 = 5-7, World 2 = 6-8, World 3 = 7-9, World 4 = 7-11, Citadel = 19
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect each stage's difficultyResult for 'bossHP' check
Verify actual HP and expected range are reported per world
criteria.threshold: Each stage result has difficultyResult.bossHP with actual value, expected range, and PASS/FAIL

criteria.description: Boss HP validation reads the actual boss maxHealth from spawnBoss() configuration for each stage
testSteps: Verify validator accesses boss health data from the boss configuration (enemies.js spawnBoss data or Level.bossData)
Verify it compares against the correct per-world HP ranges
criteria.threshold: Validator reads actual boss health from game data (not hardcoded values) and compares to difficulty table ranges

criteria.description: Validator checks enemy density per visible screen against per-world limits: World 1 max 2, World 2 max 3, World 3 max 4, World 4 max 5, Citadel max 5
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect each stage's difficultyResult for 'enemyDensity' check
Verify max enemies per screen, the world limit, and PASS/FAIL are reported
criteria.threshold: Each stage result has difficultyResult.enemyDensity with maxPerScreen, worldLimit, and PASS/FAIL

criteria.description: Enemy density check calculates max enemies visible in any single screen-width (30 tiles = 960px) window across the stage
testSteps: Review the enemy density calculation
Verify it uses a sliding window of 30 tiles (960px / 32px per tile) width
Verify it counts enemy spawns within each window and reports the maximum
criteria.threshold: Sliding window of ~30 tiles used to compute max enemies per screen

criteria.description: Validator checks hazard tile percentage against per-world limits: World 1 max 5%, World 2 max 10%, World 3 max 15%, World 4 max 20%, Citadel max 20%
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect each stage's difficultyResult for 'hazardPercentage' check
Verify actual percentage, world limit, and PASS/FAIL are reported
criteria.threshold: Each stage result has difficultyResult.hazardPercentage with actual, limit, and PASS/FAIL

criteria.description: Hazard tile percentage counts all hazard tiles (spikes, lava) relative to total non-empty tiles in the stage
testSteps: Review the hazard percentage calculation
Verify it counts TILE_HAZARD (and/or TILE_LAVA) tiles
Verify it divides by total non-empty (non-zero) tiles to produce a percentage
criteria.threshold: Hazard percentage = hazard tile count / total non-empty tile count * 100

criteria.description: Validator checks minimum safe platform width per world: World 1 min 3 tiles, World 2 min 2, World 3 min 2, World 4 min 1, Citadel min 1
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect each stage's difficultyResult for 'minPlatformWidth' check
Verify the narrowest platform found, the world minimum, and PASS/FAIL
criteria.threshold: Each stage result has difficultyResult.minPlatformWidth with narrowest, worldMin, and PASS/FAIL

criteria.description: Platform width check scans all contiguous horizontal runs of solid/one-way tiles that a player can stand on and reports the narrowest
testSteps: Review the platform width scanning logic
Verify it identifies contiguous horizontal runs of standable tiles (solid, one-way)
Verify it reports the minimum width found across all platforms in the stage
criteria.threshold: Code scans all rows for contiguous standable tile runs and tracks the minimum width

criteria.description: Each stage in the JSON report has clearly labeled check categories: pathResult, arenaResult, difficultyResult
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect the first stage result object
Verify it contains pathResult (from sprint 13), arenaResult (new), and difficultyResult (new) as top-level keys
criteria.threshold: Each stage result has pathResult, arenaResult, and difficultyResult as distinct labeled sections

criteria.description: arenaResult contains sub-checks: enclosure, minWidth, platforms, entry — each with result (PASS/FAIL) and details
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect arenaResult of any stage
Verify enclosure, minWidth, platforms, entry sub-objects each have result and details fields
criteria.threshold: arenaResult has 4 named sub-checks, each with result and details

criteria.description: difficultyResult contains sub-checks: vulnerabilityWindow, bossHP, enemyDensity, hazardPercentage, minPlatformWidth — each with result (PASS/FAIL) and details
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect difficultyResult of any stage
Verify all 5 sub-checks exist with result and details fields
criteria.threshold: difficultyResult has 5 named sub-checks, each with result and details

criteria.description: The top-level report includes aggregate counts for all check categories (path, arena, difficulty) — not just path pass/fail
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Inspect the top-level report object
Verify it includes aggregate counts that reflect all check types
criteria.threshold: Top-level report has passCount/failCount reflecting all checks (path + arena + difficulty), or separate category counts

criteria.description: Debug overlay displays arena validation results alongside path results for each stage
testSteps: Press D on title screen to open debug overlay
Verify arena check results (enclosure, width, platforms, entry) are visible in the overlay
criteria.threshold: Arena validation results displayed in the debug overlay for each stage

criteria.description: Debug overlay displays difficulty parameter check results for each stage
testSteps: Open debug overlay
Verify difficulty checks (vulnerability window, boss HP, enemy density, hazard %, platform width) are visible
criteria.threshold: Difficulty parameter results displayed in the debug overlay

criteria.description: Debug overlay uses design spec colors: Moss Green (#5A9E6F) for PASS, Ember Red (#D94F4F) for FAIL across all check categories
testSteps: Review the debug overlay rendering code for arena and difficulty result display
Verify PASS uses Moss Green and FAIL uses Ember Red
criteria.threshold: All new check categories use the same PASS/FAIL color scheme as sprint 13

criteria.description: Debug overlay summary line at the top shows total pass/fail counts across all check categories
testSteps: Open debug overlay
Verify the summary line reflects totals from path + arena + difficulty checks
criteria.threshold: Summary line counts include all check categories, not just path validation

criteria.description: Path validation from sprint 13 still works — all 13 stages have pathResult in the report
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Verify each of the 13 stages still has a pathResult field with PASS or FAIL
criteria.threshold: All 13 stages have pathResult field, BFS pathfinding still operational

criteria.description: window.runPlayabilityCheck() still returns results for all 13 stages (1-1 through 5-1)
testSteps: Call window.runPlayabilityCheck()
Verify stages array length is 13
criteria.threshold: 13 stage results with correct stage IDs

criteria.description: Game still runs at 60fps after adding arena and difficulty validation code
testSteps: Play a stage and check fps via window.__gameDebug.fps
criteria.threshold: FPS >= 55 during normal gameplay

criteria.description: Debug overlay still opens with D key on title screen and closes with Escape
testSteps: On title screen press D to open overlay
Press Escape to close
Verify title screen resumes normally
criteria.threshold: D opens overlay, Escape closes it, title screen works after

criteria.description: Title screen menu navigation and game start still work normally
testSteps: On title screen use arrow keys to navigate menu
Press Enter to start game
Verify world map loads
criteria.threshold: Title screen navigation and game start unaffected

criteria.description: All new validation code is contained within src/validator.js — no modifications to core gameplay files (player.js, physics.js, enemies.js, level.js) except read-only data access
testSteps: Review git diff for sprint 14
Verify changes are primarily in src/validator.js
Verify no behavioral changes to gameplay files
criteria.threshold: New validation logic is self-contained in validator.js; any other file changes are minimal and non-behavioral

outOfScope: Fixing stages/arenas that fail validation checks (Sprint 15)
Adjusting boss vulnerability windows or HP (Sprint 15)
Reducing enemy density or hazard percentages (Sprint 16)
Widening platforms that fail minimum width checks (Sprint 16)
notes: This sprint ONLY adds validation/reporting capabilities to the existing PlayabilityValidator. It does NOT fix any issues the validator discovers. The validator is expected to report FAIL for some checks — that is correct behavior indicating issues that Sprint 15 and 16 will address. The key deliverable is that the validator accurately detects and reports all arena and difficulty compliance issues.
